**Setup:** A pool with two floating hoops (like basketball hoops) at each end, mounted 8 feet above the water. Trampolines are positioned at the pool's edges and on floating platforms.

**Rules:**
- Two teams of 5 players each
- Players swim to move around the pool carrying a water-resistant ball
- To score, players must launch themselves off a trampoline platform, perform at least one identifiable gymnastics move in the air (tuck, pike, twist, etc.), and release the ball through the floating hoop
- A basic shot = 2 points; a shot with a recognized skill (backflip, twist) = 3 points; a shot from beyond mid-pool with a skill = 4 points
- Defense can block shots but cannot touch opponents underwater
- Possession changes after each score or if the ball is dunked underwater by a defender
- Games are four 8-minute quarters

**Key Skills Required:** Competitive swimming speed, gymnastics aerial awareness and body control, basketball shooting accuracy and court vision

The sport rewards athletic versatility and creates spectacular aerial moments over water, combining endurance, artistry, and precision.

**TERRA ARCHERY RUGBY (TAR)**

TAR combines Rugby, Archery, and Soccer.

**Setup:** A large grass field (120m x 80m) with a soccer-style goal at each end. Each goal has a bullseye target mounted in the center of the net. Teams of 7 players each.

**Rules:**
- Players advance an oversized foam ball by running and passing backward (rugby-style) OR kicking it forward (soccer-style)
- Each team has one designated "Archer" who carries a foam-tipped bow and arrow and stays in a rear zone
- Scoring:
  - Carrying/passing the ball across the opponent's goal line = 3 points (try)
  - Kicking the ball into the goal net = 2 points
  - The Archer firing a foam arrow and hitting the bullseye target on the opponent's goal from behind the halfway line = 5 points (but only allowed once every 3 minutes)
- Defenders can tackle ball carriers (rugby rules) but cannot interfere with the Archer during a shot
- If the Archer's arrow is intercepted mid-air by a defender, possession reverses
- Games are two 20-minute halves

**Key Skills Required:** Rugby physicality and passing, soccer footwork and shooting accuracy, archery precision under pressure

TAR rewards teams that balance brute ground advancement with long-range precision strikes, creating dramatic momentum swings when an Archer lands a high-value shot.

**VELODROME FENCING POLO (VFP)**

VFP combines Cycling, Fencing, and Polo.

**Setup:** An oval velodrome track (400m) with a central grass infield. Two goals (like polo goals) stand at opposite ends of the infield. Teams of 4 players each, all riding bicycles.

**Rules:**
- Players ride bikes at all times — no dismounting
- Each player carries a modified fencing épée (padded, flexible, scoring-sensor equipped)
- A lightweight rubber ball is advanced across the infield toward the opponent's goal
- Players use their épée to strike the ball along the ground (polo-style mallet role) OR to "touch" opponents on the torso to force a 10-second penalty stop (fencing scoring system)
- Scoring:
  - Hitting the ball through the opponent's goal posts = 3 points
  - Landing 3 consecutive fencing touches on the same opponent in one possession = 2 points bonus
  - Racing a full velodrome lap faster than all opponents during a designated "sprint phase" (called once per half) = 1 point
- Players tagged twice in one minute must rotate to a reserve bench for 30 seconds
- Games are two 15-minute halves

**Key Skills Required:** Cycling speed and bike handling, fencing precision and tactical awareness, polo-style striking accuracy and spatial positioning

VFP rewards athletes who can simultaneously manage speed, combat timing, and ball control, producing fast, unpredictable gameplay with constant role-switching between aggressor and defender.

**SKYBALL SLALOM**

Skyball Slalom combines Volleyball, Skiing/Snowboarding, and Tennis.

**Setup:** A snow-covered slope divided into two halves by a tall net (5 feet high) stretched across the hill. Slalom gates are staggered down both sides of the slope. Teams of 3 players each, all on skis or snowboards.

**Rules:**
- Players must always be moving downhill through at least one slalom gate before they can hit the ball
- A tennis-sized pressurized ball is served overhead (volleyball-style) across the net to start each rally
- Players hit the ball using open-palm strikes (no rackets) — volleys, spikes, and sets are all legal
- The ball must bounce once on the snow before the receiving team returns it (tennis bounce rule)
- Scoring:
  - Winning a rally = 1 point (ball lands out, hits the net, or team fails to return it)
  - If a player completes a full slalom run through all gates while their team wins the rally = bonus 2 points
  - A one-handed spike while passing through a gate = "Gate Slam" worth 3 points
- After each rally, all players ride a surface lift back to the top
- First team to 21 points wins the set; best of 3 sets wins the match

**Key Skills Required:** Skiing/snowboarding agility through gates, volleyball setting and spiking, tennis-style timing and court positioning

Skyball Slalom rewards explosive athletes who can read the ball while navigating terrain, blending aerial volleyball instincts with precision slope racing and hand-eye coordination under constant motion.

**DRIFT LACROSSE BOXING (DLB)**

DLB combines Lacrosse, Competitive Swimming, and Boxing.

**Setup:** A 50-meter pool divided into three lanes per team. Two floating goals (lacrosse-net style, 4ft wide) anchor each end just above the waterline. Teams of 6 players wearing padded aqua-boxing gloves and carrying short-handled lacrosse sticks.

**Rules:**
- Players swim continuously — no standing or resting on pool edges during play
- The ball (water polo-sized, foam) is scooped, cradled, and passed using lacrosse sticks
- To score, a player must shoot the ball into the opponent's floating goal = 2 points
- **Boxing element:** Players may challenge an opponent to a "Water Duel" — both drop their sticks onto floating platforms and exchange up to 3 padded punches (torso only). The winner of the duel (landed most clean hits, judged by sideline referee) earns their team possession and a 1-point bonus
- Duels are voluntary but can only be initiated by the player WITHOUT the ball
- A lacrosse-style behind-the-back scoop shot into the goal = 4 points ("Trick Shot")
- Dropped sticks during a duel are retrieved by teammates, creating fast-break opportunities
- Games are four 6-minute quarters; unlimited substitutions between quarters

**Key Skills Required:** Competitive swimming endurance, lacrosse stick-handling and shooting accuracy, boxing technique and composure under physical pressure

DLB rewards versatile athletes who can dominate in water, handle precision equipment, and stay composed in direct physical confrontation — creating dramatic momentum swings whenever a duel is called mid-possession.

**PARKOUR CURLING DARTS (PCD)**

PCD combines Parkour, Curling, and Darts.

**Setup:** A large indoor arena with a smooth concrete floor featuring a curling-style target (concentric rings, 12 feet diameter) painted at one end. An obstacle course of walls, platforms, vaults, and rails spans the 30-meter approach zone. Teams of 4 players each.

**Rules:**
- Each round, one player is the "Thrower" and three are "Sweepers"
- The Thrower must complete a designated parkour obstacle course (vaulting, climbing, jumping) before releasing a weighted disc (like a curling stone but flat, 2kg) toward the target rings
- While the disc slides, the three Sweepers run alongside and use padded brooms to influence its path (curling-style sweeping) — they must also clear one small hurdle mid-sweep or their sweeping is nullified
- Scoring mirrors darts: bullseye = 50 pts, inner ring = 25 pts, outer rings = 10/5/2 pts
- Teams alternate throws; each player throws twice per game
- **Parkour Bonus:** If the Thrower executes a backflip or 360-spin during the course before releasing, the disc's score is doubled
- **Sabotage Rule:** One opposing player may attempt to distract the Sweepers by racing through the obstacle course simultaneously — if they beat the Sweepers' time, sweeping effect is halved
- First team to 501 points wins (dart scoring tradition)

**Key Skills Required:** Parkour athleticism and body control, curling strategy and sweeping coordination, darts-style precision and scoring calculation under fatigue

PCD rewards teams with tactical depth — knowing when to attempt risky parkour bonuses, when to deploy the sabotage runner, and how to sweep a disc to a precise ring after an exhausting obstacle run.

**SHADOW POLO TENNIS (SPT)**

SPT combines Rock Climbing, Water Polo, and Tennis.

**Setup:** A large indoor facility with a climbing wall on each end (20ft high, 30ft wide) rising above a deep pool. A tennis net stretches across the middle of the pool at 4 feet above the water surface. Teams of 4 players each.

**Rules:**
- Players swim to move around the pool; the ball is a pressurized foam tennis ball
- Players hit the ball over the net using open-palm strikes or closed-fist punches (no rackets)
- The ball must clear the net and land in the opponent's half of the pool to stay in play — one bounce on the water allowed before return (tennis bounce rule)
- **Climbing element:** Each team has a designated "Wall Zone" at their end. If a player climbs the wall and tags a colored hold above 15ft before returning to the water, their team earns a **POWER PLAY** — for 60 seconds, any shot that strikes the opponent's wall face directly = 3 bonus points
- Standard rally win = 1 point
- Spiking the ball downward from a jump out of the water = 2 points ("Surge Spike")
- Climbing the wall and releasing the ball from height into the opponent's pool zone = 4 points ("Summit Drop") — defenders may intercept mid-air
- If a climber falls into the pool before tagging the target hold, possession transfers immediately
- Games are best of 5 sets; first to 11 points per set

**Key Skills Required:** Competitive swimming stamina, tennis-style hand-eye coordination and court positioning, rock climbing grip strength and route-reading under fatigue

SPT creates constant strategic tension — teams must decide when to sacrifice a swimmer for a wall climb bonus, defend against aerial Summit Drops, and maintain rally consistency while exhausted in water.

**MOTO DISC WRESTLING (MDW)**

MDW combines Skateboarding, Ultimate Frisbee, and Sumo Wrestling.

**Setup:** A circular arena (40-meter diameter) with a smooth concrete surface featuring a half-pipe ramp on each side and a central "Clash Zone" (a padded 8-meter circle). Two elevated disc targets (like large hoops, 3ft diameter) are mounted 10ft high on opposite ends. Teams of 5 players on skateboards at all times.

**Rules:**
- Players skate continuously to move; no stopping or foot-dragging except in the Clash Zone
- A standard disc (frisbee) is advanced by passing only — no skating with the disc more than 2 seconds
- Scoring:
  - Throwing the disc through the opponent's elevated hoop = 3 points
  - A disc thrown from the top of the half-pipe ramp = 5 points ("Ramp Bomb")
  - Turnovers occur on incomplete passes, out-of-bounds, or held disc over 2 seconds
- **Sumo Element:** Any player may challenge an opponent in the Clash Zone to a "Board Brawl" — both skate into the zone and attempt to push the other off their board or out of the circle (no punching; shoulder/body pushing only). Winner gains possession and a free uncontested pass
- Board Brawl loser sits out 45 seconds
- A between-the-legs disc release during a ramp launch = "Trick Toss," doubling the point value
- Games are three 10-minute periods

**Key Skills Required:** Skateboarding balance and ramp technique, ultimate frisbee throwing precision and field vision, sumo-style leverage and core strength

MDW rewards players who blend aerial disc artistry with aggressive physical dominance, creating explosive momentum shifts whenever a Clash Zone duel flips possession mid-attack.

**TIDAL SIEGE ARCHATHLON (TSA)**

TSA combines Track & Field (Heptathlon-style), Rowing, and Dodgeball.

**Setup:** A river or large lake channel (200m long, 40m wide) flanked by two running tracks (100m each) along the banks. Each team has a fortified floating platform "fortress" at their end. Teams of 6 players — 3 in boats, 3 on the running track at all times.

**Rules:**
- The 3 boat players row toward the opponent's fortress using single-scull oars; the 3 track players sprint alongside on the bank
- Both boat and track players carry foam dodgeballs
- **Dodgeball element:** Track players hurl dodgeballs at opposing rowers to "stun" them — a hit forces that rower to stop for 8 seconds. Rowers can deflect with their oar (counts as a block)
- **Track & Field element:** At three designated "Throw Zones" along the bank, track players must complete a standing long jump before releasing a dodgeball — throws without the jump are invalid
- **Scoring:**
  - A rower reaching and tagging the opponent's fortress = 5 points
  - A track player completing a 100m sprint faster than all opponents in that round = 2 points
  - Hitting 3 consecutive rowers in one possession = "Siege Bonus" worth 3 points
- Roles swap (boat ↔ track) every 5 minutes
- Games are four 10-minute quarters

**Key Skills Required:** Rowing power and technique, sprinting and jumping athleticism, dodgeball accuracy and evasion

TSA rewards teams that coordinate land and water attacks simultaneously, punishing opponents who focus defense in only one zone.

**GRAVITY CHESS RALLY (GCR)**

GCR combines Motorsport Rally Racing, Chess, and Gymnastics.

**Setup:** An indoor arena with a large floor grid (8x8 squares, each 4m wide — a giant chessboard). Each square contains a mini trampoline or gymnastics apparatus (vault, beam section, spring floor panel). Two teams of 4 players each wear color-coded suits. A digital overhead scoreboard tracks piece positions.

**Rules:**
- Each player is assigned a chess piece role (King, Queen, Rook, Bishop — 4 per team) with movement rules matching chess (Bishop moves diagonally, Rook moves in straight lines, etc.)
- Players physically move between squares by riding small electric karts along designated track lanes bordering the grid — they must complete a timed circuit lap before "moving" to a new square (motorsport element)
- Upon landing on a new square, the player must perform the gymnastics skill assigned to that square (e.g., trampoline tuck jump, vault landing, balance beam walk) — failure to complete the skill sends them back to their previous square
- **Capturing:** When two players occupy adjacent squares, they enter a "Speed Duel" — both race one kart lap; the faster player captures the opponent, who sits out 2 minutes
- Scoring:
  - Capturing an opponent's piece = 1 point
  - Moving your King to the opponent's back row = 5 points ("Checkmate Run")
  - Completing a gymnastics skill with a judge-rated difficulty bonus = +1 point
- Games are 25 minutes; highest score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Kart driving precision and race-line strategy, chess spatial planning and piece coordination, gymnastics technique and composure under competitive pressure

GCR rewards athletes who can think tactically like chess grandmasters, drive aggressively under time pressure, and execute clean gymnastics skills while fatigued — creating a uniquely cerebral yet physically explosive competition.

**CANOPY HANDBALL BIATHLON (CHB)**

CHB combines Handball, Cross-Country Skiing, and Rock Climbing.

**Setup:** An outdoor winter course (2km loop) through forested terrain featuring two climbing walls (25ft high) at each end of the loop and a handball goal (3m x 2m) mounted at the base of each wall. Teams of 5 players on cross-country skis at all times.

**Rules:**
- Players ski the loop carrying a foam handball, passing to teammates (no skating with the ball more than 3 seconds)
- To score, a player must first scale the climbing wall to the top, tag a bell, then descend and shoot the ball into the goal within 10 seconds of landing — 3 points
- A shot without climbing = 1 point, but only valid from beyond a marked long-range line
- **Biathlon element:** At two "Rifle Stations" along the ski loop, players may stop and throw the handball at a 60cm target 15 meters away. A hit freezes one opponent for 20 seconds; a miss adds a 30-second penalty loop for the thrower
- Defenders may block climbing by tagging a climber's ankle (legal body contact only on the wall) — tagged climbers must restart the climb
- Possession transfers on incomplete passes, out-of-bounds, or held ball over 3 seconds
- Games are two 20-minute halves; unlimited rolling substitutions

**Key Skills Required:** Cross-country skiing endurance and technique, handball throwing accuracy and team passing, rock climbing grip strength and speed under fatigue

CHB rewards teams that balance skiing stamina, precise target throwing at rifle stations, and the courage to commit a player to the wall climb — creating high-stakes decisions as defenders race to intercept climbers before they descend for a shot.

**SURGE KENDO RELAY (SKR)**

SKR combines Competitive Swimming, Kendo, and Track & Field Relay Racing.

**Setup:** An Olympic-size pool (50m) with a 200m running track wrapping around it. At each end of the pool, a raised platform (3ft above water) holds two "Gate Targets" — padded sensor panels. Teams of 4 players each.

**Rules:**
- Each round is a relay: Player 1 sprints 100m on the track, tags Player 2, who swims 50m across the pool, who tags Player 3, who sprints 100m back, who tags Player 4 — the "Striker"
- The Striker, armed with a padded shinai (kendo bamboo sword) and wearing light kendo armor, faces an opponent Striker in a 60-second kendo duel on the raised platform above the pool
- Kendo scoring: clean strikes to the head (men), wrist (kote), or torso (do) = 1 point each, judged by sensors in the armor
- The team whose Striker scores more kendo points in the duel wins the round = 3 points; a tie = 1 point each
- **Speed Bonus:** If a team completes the relay leg 5+ seconds faster than opponents, their Striker gets a free uncontested strike before the duel begins
- **Knockback Rule:** A Striker knocked off the platform into the pool loses the duel immediately — opponent wins 3 points automatically
- A full kendo disarm (shinai knocked away) = instant round win worth 5 points
- Match is 6 rounds; highest cumulative score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Sprint relay speed and baton-tag precision, competitive swimming technique, kendo striking accuracy and balance under pressure

SKR rewards teams with both athletic depth across disciplines and a dominant Striker — creating dramatic finishes where a slow relay leg can be overturned by a decisive kendo disarm.

**DUNE POLO VAULT TENNIS (DPVT)**

DPVT combines Beach Volleyball, Pole Vaulting, and Table Tennis.

**Setup:** A sand court (20m x 10m) with a standard volleyball net (8ft high) across the center. On each side, two padded vaulting runways (15m long) run parallel to the sidelines, each with a lowered crossbar (6ft) over a sand pit. Teams of 3 players each.

**Rules:**
- A ping-pong-sized pressurized foam ball is used; players hit it with open-palm strikes (no paddles, no rackets)
- Standard rally play: the ball must clear the net and land in the opponent's half — one bounce on sand allowed (table tennis bounce rule applied to a large court)
- **Vaulting element:** A player may grab a flexible fiberglass pole (stored courtside) and vault over the crossbar before striking the ball. A vaulted strike = "Aerial Smash" and counts double points if it wins the rally
- Scoring mirrors table tennis: rallies scored every point, games to 11, best of 5 games per set
- **Spike Rule:** A player who successfully vaults and spikes the ball straight down into the opponent's court before it clears the net = "Vault Spike" worth 3 points
- If a vaulter knocks the crossbar off during the jump, they lose the rally automatically
- A behind-the-back palm strike without vaulting = "Sand Flick," worth 2 points if it wins the rally
- Substitutions allowed between games only

**Key Skills Required:** Beach volleyball court awareness and sand movement, pole vaulting approach speed and bar clearance timing, table tennis-style spin reading and rapid reflexes

DPVT rewards players who master the risk-reward decision of committing to a vault — sacrificing positioning for a high-value aerial strike — while maintaining the lightning-fast rally instincts of table tennis on an uneven sand surface.

**GLACIER KABADDI BIKEATHLON (GKB)**

GKB combines Kabaddi, Mountain Biking, and Javelin Throw.

**Setup:** A rugged outdoor course (1km circuit) with varied terrain — dirt trails, log jumps, and a central "Raid Arena" (a flat 20m x 15m zone). A javelin target wall (3m wide, sensor-equipped rings) stands at each end of the course. Teams of 6 players on mountain bikes at all times.

**Rules:**
- **Kabaddi element:** One player per team is designated the "Raider" each round. The Raider bikes into the opponent's half of the Raid Arena, attempts to tag as many defenders as possible, and must return to their half while chanting continuously ("kabaddi, kabaddi...") — if they stop chanting or are physically blocked by two or more defenders holding them, they are eliminated for 90 seconds
- Each successful tag by the Raider = 1 point; returning safely after tagging = 1 bonus point
- **Javelin element:** At two "Launch Zones" along the course, players may grab a foam-tipped javelin and hurl it at the target wall while still mounted on their bike (moving or stationary). Bullseye = 4 points, outer rings = 2/1 points
- **Mountain Biking element:** A timed circuit lap is run simultaneously — the team that completes the most laps in a 5-minute window earns 3 bonus points per round
- Defenders knocked off their bikes by a Raider contact = eliminated for 60 seconds
- A Raider who tags 3+ defenders in one raid = "Rampage," worth double points for that raid
- Games are four 8-minute quarters; roles rotate each quarter

**Key Skills Required:** Kabaddi breath control and evasion instincts, mountain biking technical handling and endurance, javelin accuracy and power while in motion

GKB rewards teams that coordinate simultaneous lap racing, strategic raiding, and precision javelin throws — punishing opponents who commit all defenders to stopping the Raider while leaving the circuit and launch zones uncontested.

**TRENCH BOWLS FREERUN (TBF)**

TBF combines American Football, Bowling, and Parkour.

**Setup:** A rectangular indoor arena (60m x 30m) with a textured rubber floor. Each end zone contains a giant pin formation — 6 oversized weighted pins (2ft tall) arranged in a triangle. Scattered throughout the field are parkour obstacles: walls, rails, platforms, and gap jumps at varying heights. Teams of 5 players each.

**Rules:**
- Players advance a foam football toward the opponent's end zone by running and passing (no backward pass restriction — any direction allowed)
- Ball carrier may not be tackled; instead, defenders "tag" with two hands to force an immediate pass within 2 seconds or possession transfers
- **Bowling element:** To score, a player must roll or throw the football from outside the end zone to knock down as many pins as possible. Each pin knocked down = 1 point. Knocking all 6 = "Strike" worth 10 points
- **Parkour element:** A player who passes through at least two obstacles (vaults a rail, clears a platform gap, etc.) before receiving a pass earns a "Freerun Catch" — their team gets one re-throw if their first pin attempt is unsatisfactory
- A between-the-legs throw that knocks 4+ pins = "Trick Bowl," doubling that throw's score
- Defenders may block pin throws by standing in the end zone, but must have completed one obstacle crossing in that possession to be eligible to defend
- Games are four 8-minute quarters; possession alternates after each pin attempt

**Key Skills Required:** Football throwing accuracy and field vision, bowling precision and spin control under pressure, parkour agility and obstacle navigation at speed

TBF rewards teams that create fluid parkour-assisted passing routes to set up clean pin approaches, while defenders must stay athletically active through obstacles just to earn the right to protect their pins.

**TIDE GATE PENTATHLON (TGP)**

TGP combines Surfing, Fencing, and Cross-Country Running.

**Setup:** A coastal course beginning at a beach break (surf zone, 200m wide), transitioning to a 1km sand/trail running path, and ending at an indoor "Duel Deck" — a raised platform (15m x 6m) with non-slip matting overlooking the water. Teams of 4 players each.

**Rules:**
- Each round begins with all players paddling out and riding one wave on a shortboard. A panel of 2 judges scores each ride (1–10 scale); the team with the higher combined surf score earns a **Tide Advantage** (their fencers get a 2-point head start in duels that round)
- After surfing, players run the 1km trail course carrying their fencing épée (padded, sensor-equipped). The first player from each team to arrive at the Duel Deck triggers the round's fencing phase
- **Fencing element:** Arriving players immediately duel — first to 5 sensor-confirmed touches wins the duel = 3 points for their team. Subsequent arriving players queue and duel in order of arrival
- A player who arrives at the Duel Deck in 1st place overall (fastest across both teams) gets one free uncontested strike before their duel begins
- **Surf Trick Bonus:** A player who executes an aerial maneuver during their wave ride earns their team a "Ghost Touch" — one phantom point added to their fencing score automatically
- Wiping out on the wave = player must re-paddle and catch a second wave before running; no surf score awarded
- Match is 4 rounds; highest cumulative points wins

**Key Skills Required:** Surfing wave-reading and aerial technique, trail running speed and endurance while carrying equipment, fencing composure and precision after physical exhaustion

TGP rewards athletes who can dominate the ocean, recover quickly on land, and execute sharp fencing technique while oxygen-depleted — creating dramatic reversals when a superior surfer arrives late to the Duel Deck and must overcome a fencing deficit with pure skill.

**ROOFTOP CRICKET JUDO (RCJ)**

RCJ combines Cricket, Judo, and Skateboarding.

**Setup:** A large flat concrete arena (50m x 30m) with a smooth skating surface. Three cricket wickets stand at each end. Scattered across the field are quarter-pipe ramps and grinding rails. Teams of 5 players each, all on skateboards at all times.

**Rules:**
- A bowler skates toward the crease and launches a foam ball (cricket-style) at the opponent's wickets
- The "Batter" uses a flat paddle (cricket bat shape, shortened) to deflect the ball and skate to score runs between the two wicket ends
- **Scoring:**
  - Reaching the opposite wicket end and returning = 1 run
  - Hitting the ball off a quarter-pipe ramp and over the boundary line = 6 runs ("Ramp Six")
  - Ball striking the wickets = batter is OUT
- **Judo element:** Fielders may initiate a "Board Grapple" — skating alongside the batter and attempting a judo-style hip throw or shoulder throw to knock them off their board. A successful throw = batter is OUT and the throwing fielder's team earns a bonus 2 runs
- Batter may brace and resist the throw — if they stay on the board for 3 seconds of contact, the fielder receives a 30-second penalty and the batter earns 1 bonus run
- Only one Board Grapple attempt per delivery
- A grinding trick along a rail before bowling = "Style Bowl," and if it takes the wicket, scores 3 bonus runs for the bowling team
- Each team faces 10 deliveries per innings; highest run total wins

**Key Skills Required:** Cricket batting timing and wicket defense, judo throwing technique and balance, skateboarding speed and ramp awareness

RCJ rewards teams that mix precision bowling, explosive grappling, and aerial skating creativity — with every delivery carrying the threat of a sudden judo takedown that can flip momentum in an instant.

**IRONVEIL LACROSSE CURLING (ILC)**

ILC combines Ice Hockey, Lacrosse, and Curling.

**Setup:** A full-size ice rink divided into two halves. Each end has a curling house (target rings) painted into the ice AND a lacrosse-style goal (4ft x 4ft net) mounted 6ft above the ice on a post directly behind the curling house. Teams of 5 players each on ice skates, each carrying a hybrid stick — one side is a lacrosse head for catching/throwing, the other is a flat curling-broom pad for sweeping.

**Rules:**
- Players skate freely; a rubber foam ball is advanced by passing and cradling with the lacrosse head (no skating with ball over 3 seconds)
- **Curling element:** To attempt a goal, the attacking player must first "deliver" the ball by sliding it along the ice (curling-release style, one knee down) toward the house. Teammates may sweep the ball's path with their stick's broom side to guide it
- Ball landing in the button (bullseye) = unlocks a **free aerial shot** — that player scoops the ball with the lacrosse head and shoots at the elevated net = 4 points
- Ball landing in outer rings = a ground-level lacrosse shot at the net = 2 points
- Ball missing the house entirely = possession transfers
- **Hockey element:** Defenders may body-check (shoulder only, no stick contact) to disrupt deliveries or intercept passes
- A behind-the-back lacrosse pass that leads directly to a button landing = "Silk Delivery," doubling that shot's value
- Games are three 12-minute periods; highest score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Ice skating agility and checking physicality, lacrosse stick-handling and shooting accuracy, curling delivery precision and sweeping strategy

ILC rewards teams that combine brute skating physicality with delicate curling precision — the tension between aggressive checking and the need for a controlled, graceful delivery creates a uniquely strategic and spectacular sport.

**SANDSTORM ARCHERY DODGEBALL (SAD)**

SAD combines Beach Volleyball, Archery, and Dodgeball.

**Setup:** A sand court (25m x 15m) divided by a 5-foot barrier wall (players cannot cross it). Each half has two "Archer Towers" — raised platforms (4ft high) at the back corners. Two foam target shields (3ft diameter, sensor-equipped) hang at mid-court height on each side. Teams of 6 players each — 4 "Court Players" and 2 "Archers" stationed on towers.

**Rules:**
- Court Players throw foam dodgeballs (3 per side) at opponents across the barrier wall — a hit on an opponent = that player freezes for 10 seconds
- Archers on towers fire foam-tipped arrows at the opponent's hanging target shields. Hitting a shield = 3 points AND unfreezes any frozen teammate instantly
- **Volleyball element:** Court Players may volley dodgeballs overhead (volleyball-style set or spike) to loft them over the barrier in high arcs — a ball that lands in the opponent's back zone (behind a marked line) scores 1 point regardless of hitting anyone
- A "Block Spike" — a Court Player leaping to intercept an incoming arcing ball and spiking it back = 2 points if it lands in bounds
- Archers may switch with Court Players only between rounds
- Hitting an Archer on their tower with a dodgeball = Archer must step down for 30 seconds, leaving their team with one tower
- A simultaneous arrow hit AND dodgeball freeze in the same second = "Sandstorm Strike" worth 6 points
- Games are four 7-minute quarters; team with most points wins

**Key Skills Required:** Volleyball leaping and spiking instincts, archery accuracy under crowd pressure, dodgeball throwing speed and evasion

SAD rewards teams that coordinate ground-level dodgeball pressure with elevated archery precision — forcing opponents to choose between shielding their Court Players or protecting their Archers, with every Sandstorm Strike capable of erasing a multi-point deficit instantly.

**NEON GATE WRESTLING RELAY (NGWR)**

NGWR combines Competitive Swimming, Freestyle Wrestling, and Slalom Canoe/Kayak.

**Setup:** A 150m river-style channel (can be artificial whitewater) with 12 hanging slalom gates across its length. At the midpoint sits a raised "Clash Platform" (8m x 6m, padded, just above waterline). Each end has a submerged touch-pad finish wall. Teams of 4 players each in single kayaks.

**Rules:**
- Players paddle kayaks through the slalom gates — missing or hitting a gate adds a 5-second time penalty
- At the midpoint, one designated "Wrestler" from each team beaches their kayak, climbs the Clash Platform, and engages in a 45-second freestyle wrestling bout (standard takedown rules, no pins — points only)
- While wrestlers compete, remaining teammates continue paddling gates and may lap the course for bonus points
- **Scoring:**
  - Completing the full course (all gates clean) = 5 points
  - Each wrestling takedown scored on the platform = 2 points
  - Throwing the opponent OFF the platform into the water = instant 6-point "Surge Throw" and opponent loses 30 seconds re-boarding their kayak
  - Team whose combined paddling time (all 4 members) is fastest each half = 3 bonus points
- Wrestlers must re-board and finish the course after the bout; their gate penalties still count
- A wrestler who completes the course after a Surge Throw win with zero gate penalties = "Iron Run," worth 4 bonus points
- Games are two 20-minute halves; roles rotate each half

**Key Skills Required:** Kayak paddling precision and gate navigation, freestyle wrestling leverage and explosive takedown technique, swimming recovery speed when capsized

NGWR rewards teams that field a dominant wrestler without sacrificing paddling speed — creating brutal strategic dilemmas when your best wrestler is also your fastest kayaker, and every Surge Throw ripples across the scoreboard like a wave.

**EMBER TRACK POLO VAULT (ETPV)**

ETPV combines Horse Racing, Handball, and High Jump.

**Setup:** An oval dirt track (600m circuit) with a central grass infield. Two handball goals (3m x 2m) stand at opposite ends of the infield. Along the infield perimeter, six "Leap Stations" are marked — each containing a padded high jump bar set at 1.5m. Teams of 5 players each riding horses at all times.

**Rules:**
- Players ride horses across the infield and around the track, advancing a foam handball by passing only — no carrying the ball while galloping more than 4 seconds
- **Handball element:** Players throw and catch the ball with one hand while mounted; a shot into the opponent's goal = 3 points. Defenders may intercept passes but no physical contact with riders
- **High Jump element:** Before any player is eligible to shoot on goal, at least one teammate must have successfully guided their horse over a Leap Station bar in that possession. A cleared bar "activates" the goal for 20 seconds. Knocking the bar down = possession transfers immediately
- **Racing element:** Once per half, a "Sprint Flag" is raised — all players abandon the ball and race one full track circuit. The team placing two or more riders in the top three finishers earns 4 bonus points and resets all Leap Station bars to a higher height (1.8m) for the next 5 minutes
- A one-handed behind-the-back pass leading directly to a goal = "Gallop Trick," doubling that goal's value
- Games are two 18-minute halves; highest score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Equestrian control and racing speed, handball throwing accuracy and court vision while mounted, horse-and-rider coordination over jump obstacles under competitive pressure

ETPV rewards riders who combine raw racing speed, aerial jumping precision, and sharp passing instincts — with every Sprint Flag moment reshuffling the tactical landscape and every knocked bar threatening to hand momentum to the opposition.

**VOLCANO DISC LUGE (VDL)**

VDL combines Disc Golf, Luge/Skeleton, and Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu.

**Setup:** A winding downhill course (800m) carved into a hillside with a smooth concrete track. Along the route, 9 "Disc Baskets" (standard disc golf chain targets) are mounted at varying heights beside the track. At three "Grapple Zones" — flat padded sections interrupting the course — players must stop briefly. Teams of 4 players each riding flat sliding boards (luge-style, feet-first).

**Rules:**
- Players slide downhill on their boards, carrying a standard disc golf disc
- They must throw their disc at each basket they pass — a catch in the chains = 2 points; a miss = 0 and a 4-second time penalty added to their run
- **Luge element:** Each player's total course time is recorded. The team with the lowest combined time across all 4 runs each round earns 5 bonus points
- **BJJ element:** At each Grapple Zone, a player from each team dismounts and engages in a 30-second ground grappling bout (BJJ rules — submissions and positional control scored by a judge). Winner = 3 points; a submission = 5 points. Both players then re-board and continue
- A disc thrown mid-slide (without stopping) = "Velocity Toss" — worth 4 points if it catches
- A player who wins a BJJ submission AND posts the fastest individual run time in the same round = "Eruption," worth 8 bonus points
- Games are 3 rounds; highest cumulative score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Disc golf throwing accuracy at speed and odd angles, luge body positioning and course navigation for time, BJJ ground technique and submission hunting under fatigue

VDL rewards athletes who can stay precise with a disc while hurtling downhill, dominate opponents on the ground mid-course, and recover their sliding rhythm after a grappling bout — with every Velocity Toss and Eruption moment capable of overturning a comfortable lead.

**IRON CANOPY SIEGE (ICS)**

ICS combines Rock Climbing, Water Polo, and Archery.

**Setup:** A massive indoor aquatic facility with two deep pools (25m x 15m each) connected by a central "Sky Bridge" — a rope-and-platform obstacle course suspended 12ft above the water. Climbing walls (20ft high) rise from the pool edges at each end. Floating archery targets (60cm bullseye rings) are tethered at mid-pool on each side. Teams of 5 players each.

**Rules:**
- Players swim to move within their pool zone; a water polo ball is advanced by passing only (no swimming with ball over 2 seconds)
- **Water Polo element:** Throwing the ball into a standard goal net at the opponent's pool end = 2 points. Defenders may block shots and intercept passes
- **Climbing element:** A player may exit the water and scale their end's climbing wall. Reaching the top and ringing a bell earns their team a "High Ground Token" — redeemable once to make any goal shot worth double. Falling off the wall = 30-second water exclusion penalty
- **Archery element:** One designated "Siege Archer" per team stands on a floating platform and fires foam-tipped arrows at the opponent's tethered floating target. Bullseye = 4 points; outer ring = 2 points. Archers may be splashed by opponents to disrupt aim but cannot be physically touched
- **Sky Bridge:** A player who crosses the Sky Bridge obstacle course and reaches the opponent's side may "raid" — intercepting one pass without penalty. Falling off the Bridge = player drops into the water on the opponent's side and must swim back
- A simultaneous climbing bell-ring AND bullseye arrow hit in the same 10-second window = "Canopy Strike" worth 7 points
- Games are four 8-minute quarters

**Key Skills Required:** Water polo swimming endurance and passing vision, rock climbing grip and speed under fatigue, archery precision on an unstable floating surface

ICS rewards teams that balance aquatic ball movement, vertical climbing commitment, and long-range archery precision — forcing opponents to defend three simultaneous threat vectors while the Sky Bridge raider lurks above, ready to disrupt everything.

**STORM GATE TRIATHLON COMBAT (SGTC)**

SGTC combines Taekwondo, Sailing, and Shot Put.

**Setup:** A large coastal bay course with three sailboat-sized catamarans per team (crewed by 2 players each). Six floating "Gate Rings" (3m diameter buoys) are anchored across the bay in a zigzag pattern. At each gate, a padded dueling platform (4m x 4m) floats alongside. A weighted foam "storm ball" (4kg, shot put size) sits on each platform. Teams of 6 players total — 2 per catamaran.

**Rules:**
- Teams race their catamarans through all six gates in sequence (sailing skill determines speed and positioning)
- At each gate, one player from each team leaps from their boat onto the dueling platform for a **Taekwondo Clash** — 30-second bout, sensor-equipped pads, kicks only (no punches). Most clean kicks wins the gate = 3 points; a spinning kick = 5 points
- **Shot Put element:** The gate winner earns the right to hurl the storm ball from the platform toward a floating target ring 15m away. Bullseye = 4 bonus points; landing in the outer ring = 2 points; a miss = 0
- The losing gate fighter must swim back to their catamaran (no ladder assist), costing precious sailing time
- **Sailing element:** The team that arrives at each gate FIRST gets a 1-point automatic advantage before the Taekwondo bout begins
- A one-legged spinning kick that wins the bout AND a bullseye throw in the same gate = "Tempest Strike" worth 10 points
- Games are two full bay circuits (all 6 gates twice); highest cumulative score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Sailing speed and tactical wind reading, Taekwondo kicking precision and balance on an unstable floating platform, shot put explosive power and distance accuracy

SGTC rewards teams with seamless transitions between sailing strategy, combat dominance, and throwing power — every gate is a self-contained battle where arriving late, losing the kick duel, or missing the throw can each independently cost the match.

**MIRAGE POLO SPRINT (MPS)**

MPS combines Camel/Horse Polo, Sprinting/Hurdles, and Synchronized Swimming.

**Setup:** A large outdoor arena (80m x 50m) with a shallow performance pool (30m x 20m, 1.5m deep) at the center, surrounded by a rubberized sprint-and-hurdles track (200m loop) with 8 hurdles per lane. Two polo-style goals (4m wide) stand at opposite ends of the pool. Teams of 5 players each — 2 "Sprinters," 2 "Pool Players," and 1 "Conductor."

**Rules:**
- Pool Players swim and advance a water polo ball through the pool, shooting into the opponent's goal = 3 points
- **Synchronized Swimming element:** Before any goal attempt is valid, the two Pool Players must perform a recognized synchronized figure (a lift, a vertical, or a spinning duet) lasting at least 4 seconds. A judge rates the figure 1–5; rating of 4+ doubles the goal's value. A rating below 2 = shot disallowed
- **Sprinting/Hurdles element:** Sprinters race the 200m hurdle loop continuously. Each completed clean lap (no knocked hurdles) earns the team 1 point and grants their Pool Players a 15-second "Free Zone" — defenders must retreat 5m
- A Sprinter who knocks a hurdle must complete a penalty lap before their laps count again
- **Conductor role:** The Conductor stands at poolside and calls the synchronized figure type before each possession. Calling a "Lift" figure and having it rated 5 = "Mirage Moment" — goal scored in that possession worth 8 points
- Defenders may intercept passes in the pool but cannot disrupt the synchronized figure once initiated
- A Pool Player who scores while performing a solo back-walkover above the waterline = "Desert Star," worth 5 points
- Games are four 8-minute quarters

**Key Skills Required:** Water polo passing and shooting accuracy, synchronized swimming coordination and artistry under pressure, sprint hurdling speed and clean technique

MPS rewards teams that fuse athletic grace with raw speed — the synchronized judging element forces competitors to be both precise artists and clinical finishers, while the Sprinters' constant lapping creates relentless tactical pressure that no amount of beautiful swimming can ignore.

**RIDGE COMBAT CURLING SPRINT (RCCS)**

RCCS combines Bobsled/Luge, Muay Thai Kickboxing, and Curling.

**Setup:** A refrigerated indoor oval track (300m) with a smooth ice surface. The track has two "Combat Pads" — raised octagonal padded platforms (6m diameter) positioned at opposite curves of the oval. At each straightaway end, a curling house (target rings, 3.6m diameter) is embedded in the ice. Teams of 4 players each riding two-person sleds.

**Rules:**
- Teams of 2 ride sleds around the oval continuously; the other 2 teammates stand at Combat Pads and curling houses on foot
- **Bobsled element:** Each sled team completes laps — every clean lap (no wall contact) = 1 point. The faster sled each lap earns a "Speed Token" used to unlock bonus throws
- **Curling element:** Foot players take turns delivering a standard curling stone toward the house. Teammates on foot may sweep. Button (bullseye) = 5 points; rings = 3/2/1 points. A Speed Token allows a second delivery that round
- **Muay Thai element:** At each Combat Pad, one player per team engages in a 40-second Muay Thai bout — padded shinguards and gloves, sensor-equipped. Judges score clean kicks, knees, and elbows (no punches to the head). Winner = 3 points; a clean body kick that staggers the opponent = 5 points ("Ridge Strike")
- Combat bout winners earn the right to "bump" one opponent's curling stone out of the house (like a takeout shot) before scoring is tallied
- A sled team that completes a lap in record time for that match AND their foot teammate wins the simultaneous Combat bout = "Full Ridge" worth 7 bonus points
- Roles rotate every 6 minutes (sled ↔ foot)
- Games are three 15-minute periods; highest score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Bobsled/luge driving precision and aerodynamic positioning, Muay Thai striking technique and balance on a padded platform, curling stone delivery and sweeping strategy

RCCS rewards teams with athletic range across speed, combat, and precision — the sled team's lap dominance directly fuels the curling phase, while Combat bout outcomes reshape the scoreboard mid-delivery, ensuring no lead is ever truly safe.

**APEX GRID COMBAT (AGC)**

AGC combines American Ninja Warrior-style Obstacle Racing, Volleyball, and Taekwondo.

**Setup:** A rectangular arena (60m x 40m) divided into three zones: a central "Grid" obstacle course (walls, balance beams, rope swings, monkey bars), and two "Court Zones" at each end with a volleyball net (8ft high) and a padded dueling circle (6m diameter). Teams of 5 players each.

**Rules:**
- A rally begins when the serving team volleys a foam ball over the net into the opponent's Court Zone — standard volleyball rules apply (3 touches max, no double hits)
- **Obstacle element:** To earn the right to SPIKE the ball, a player must first cross the Grid obstacle course and return to their Court Zone within that possession. An uncertified spike = illegal, possession transfers
- **Taekwondo element:** Once per possession, any player may issue a "Grid Challenge" — both players sprint to the dueling circle and compete in a 20-second Taekwondo bout (sensor pads, kicks only). The winner earns their team a "Power Play" — the next spike is worth double if it wins the rally
- Scoring mirrors volleyball: rally point system, games to 25, best of 5 sets
- A player who completes the obstacle course AND wins a Grid Challenge duel in the same possession = "Apex Moment" — their next spike is worth triple points
- Falling off any obstacle resets that player to the start; they cannot spike until successfully crossing
- A jump-kick performed mid-obstacle course (off a platform) that is judged "clean" by a sideline referee = "Sky Strike Bonus" — adds 1 free point to the next rally win

**Key Skills Required:** Ninja-style obstacle agility and grip endurance, volleyball setting and spiking timing, Taekwondo kicking balance and precision under cardiovascular fatigue

AGC rewards teams that can sustain volleyball precision while managing who crosses the obstacle course and who challenges duels — sending your best spiker through the Grid while keeping a Taekwondo specialist ready to flip possession creates a constantly shifting tactical chess match above the net.

**IRONSHORE BOWLS COMBAT (IBC)**

IBC combines Rowing, Boxing, and Lawn Bowls.

**Setup:** A 500m rectangular water channel (40m wide) with a smooth floating green platform (20m x 10m, like a giant lawn bowls rink) anchored at the midpoint. A padded boxing ring (6m x 6m) sits at the center of the floating platform. Two "jack" buoys are tethered at opposite ends of the channel. Teams of 4 players each in double-sculling rowboats.

**Rules:**
- Teams row their boats from one end of the channel toward the jack buoy, releasing weighted floating bowls (like lawn bowls, 1kg rubber) underarm while still moving — the bowl that stops closest to the jack = 2 points per bowl in the cluster
- **Rowing element:** Each team rows two circuits before each bowls round. The team completing both circuits fastest earns "Lane Advantage" — they deliver their bowls first and may place a blocker bowl before opponents throw
- **Boxing element:** After each bowls round, one player per team boards the floating platform for a 60-second boxing bout (headgear, sensor gloves, clean punches only). The bout winner earns the right to "knock away" one opponent bowl from the jack — removing it from scoring
- A player who wins the boxing bout AND had a bowl touching the jack = "Ironshore" worth 8 points
- Throwing a bowl while standing upright in a moving boat = "Standing Cast," worth double if it scores
- Knocked into the water during a boxing bout = opponent wins the round automatically
- Games are 6 rounds; highest cumulative score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Rowing speed and synchronization, lawn bowls precision and weight control from a moving vessel, boxing technique and footwork on an unstable floating platform

IBC rewards teams that combine raw aquatic power, delicate throwing accuracy, and combat dominance — every boxing bout reshapes the scoreboard by erasing carefully placed bowls, ensuring no lead is secure until the final cast settles.

**CIRCUIT BLADE HUNT (CBH)**

CBH combines Orienteering, Fencing, and Skateboarding.

**Setup:** A large outdoor urban park (400m x 300m) with a mapped course featuring 12 numbered "Control Stations" hidden throughout. Each station has a padded dueling strip (8m long) and a skate obstacle (rail, ramp, or gap jump) beside it. Teams of 4 players each, all on skateboards, each carrying a padded sensor-equipped foil.

**Rules:**
- Teams receive a map at the start and must navigate to all 12 Control Stations in sequence — orienteering-style route choice is entirely up to each team (no prescribed path)
- **Skateboarding element:** Upon arriving at each station, a player must successfully complete the station's skate obstacle before the station "activates." A failed trick = one retry allowed; two failures = 30-second time penalty
- **Fencing element:** Once activated, one player from each team (if both arrive within 30 seconds of each other) engages in a 45-second foil bout on the dueling strip — first to 3 touches wins the station = 4 points; loser earns 1 point for reaching the station
- If only one team reaches a station uncontested, they earn 3 points automatically after completing the skate obstacle
- A fencing touch scored immediately after dismounting a skateboard trick (within 5 seconds) = "Blade Roll," worth 5 points
- The team completing all 12 stations fastest earns 6 bonus points regardless of duel outcomes
- A player who lands a trick, wins a duel, AND hits all 3 touches with different fencing target zones (head, wrist, torso) = "Circuit Master" worth 10 points
- Match duration: 45 minutes; highest score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Orienteering map-reading and route strategy, skateboarding technical trick execution under time pressure, fencing precision and composure after physical exertion

CBH rewards teams that balance navigational intelligence, skating boldness, and fencing sharpness — choosing the fastest route means nothing if your skater bails on the obstacle, and winning every duel won't compensate for being consistently outpaced to the stations.

**DELTA SPRINT SUMO ARCHERY (DSSA)**

DSSA combines Speed Skating, Sumo Wrestling, and Archery.

**Setup:** An oval speed skating rink (400m) with a central padded "Dome Arena" (12m diameter raised platform) at the midpoint. Six archery target boards (60cm bullseye, sensor-equipped) ring the outer wall of the rink at equal intervals. Teams of 4 players each on speed skates at all times.

**Rules:**
- Players skate the oval continuously; each clean lap = 1 point for their team
- Each player carries a compact foam-tipped recurve bow and a quiver of 3 arrows
- **Archery element:** Players may fire arrows at the 6 wall targets while skating — hitting a bullseye = 3 points; outer ring = 1 point. Arrows must be fired while in motion (no stopping). Each player has 3 arrows per period; arrows are retrieved at designated reload stations
- **Sumo element:** Any player may issue a "Dome Challenge" by skating onto the central platform. An opponent must accept within 5 seconds or forfeit 2 points. On the platform, standard sumo rules apply — push opponent off the platform edge = 4 points. Both players skate off and resume after the bout
- A player who wins a Dome bout AND immediately skates to fire a bullseye within 20 seconds = "Delta Strike" worth 7 points
- Dropping your bow during a Dome bout = automatic loss of the sumo round
- Firing an arrow mid-corner (the oval's curve) = "Apex Shot" — bullseye value doubles to 6 points
- Games are three 10-minute periods; roles unrestricted

**Key Skills Required:** Speed skating endurance and cornering technique, sumo leverage and explosive pushing power on an elevated unstable surface, archery aim and breath control while moving at speed

DSSA rewards athletes who can sustain skating pace, seize Dome Challenge moments without sacrificing lap points, and land precise arrows while oxygen-depleted — with every Delta Strike capable of reversing a deficit in one electrifying sequence.

**TEMPEST POLO VAULT COMBAT (TPVC)**

TPVC combines Canoe Polo, Pole Vaulting, and Muay Thai Kickboxing.

**Setup:** A 35m x 23m outdoor pool (canoe polo dimensions) with two suspended goals (1.5m x 1m) hanging 2m above each end's waterline. Four "Launch Docks" — raised wooden platforms (1m above water) with padded vaulting runways (12m long) — extend from each side of the pool. A padded floating "Combat Raft" (6m x 4m) is anchored at the center. Teams of 5 players each in kayaks at all times.

**Rules:**
- Players paddle kayaks to advance a water polo ball, passing and shooting at the suspended goal = 2 points (standard canoe polo rules — no swimming, kayaks only)
- **Pole Vaulting element:** A player may beach their kayak on a Launch Dock, grab a padded fiberglass pole, and vault toward the goal. A vaulted shot into the goal = 5 points ("Sky Goal"). Knocking the crossbar = possession transfers. Only one vaulter active per team at a time
- **Muay Thai element:** Any player may paddle onto the Combat Raft and issue a challenge. An opponent must accept within 8 seconds or concede 2 points. Bouts last 40 seconds — sensor pads score clean kicks and knee strikes only (no punches). Winner = 3 points; a clean spinning heel kick = 5 points ("Tempest Kick"). Loser's kayak is pushed 10m back by the referee
- A player who wins a Combat Raft bout and then immediately vaults a Sky Goal in the same possession = "Tempest Surge" worth 12 points
- Capsized kayak players must right themselves before rejoining play
- Games are four 8-minute quarters; highest score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Canoe polo paddling agility and shooting accuracy, pole vaulting approach speed and aerial timing, Muay Thai kicking balance and composure on an unstable floating surface

TPVC rewards teams that coordinate water-level ball movement, high-risk aerial vaulting, and direct physical combat simultaneously — every Combat Raft challenge pulls a player from defense, every Sky Goal attempt leaves a dock unguarded, and a single Tempest Surge can erase a five-possession deficit in one breathtaking sequence.

**FROST CIRCUIT PENTATHLON (FCP)**

FCP combines Biathlon (Cross-Country Skiing + Rifle Shooting), Gymnastics, and Lacrosse.

**Setup:** A 1.5km outdoor winter course with a groomed ski track looping through forested terrain. At three "Vault Stations" along the route, a spring-floor trampoline pad and a horizontal bar are embedded into a heated shelter. A lacrosse goal (6ft x 6ft) and a rifle range (5 targets at 20m) are positioned at the course's central hub. Teams of 4 players each on cross-country skis.

**Rules:**
- Players ski the loop continuously, carrying lacrosse sticks and a foam ball
- Ball is advanced by passing only — no skiing with the ball more than 2 seconds
- **Rifle/Biathlon element:** At the central hub, players may stop and throw the lacrosse ball at 5 metal targets (biathlon-style). Each hit = 1 point; a miss adds a 20-second penalty loop. All 5 hit in one round = "Clean Sweep" worth 5 bonus points
- **Lacrosse element:** Shooting the ball into the goal (from outside a marked crease) = 3 points. A behind-the-back stick shot = "Trick Toss," worth 5 points if it scores
- **Gymnastics element:** At each Vault Station, a player may dismount skis, perform a required gymnastics skill (kip on the bar OR a trampoline tuck-pike-layout sequence), and earn a "Precision Token." Tokens are redeemed to double the next lacrosse goal's value
- A player who earns a Precision Token, hits all 5 rifle targets, AND scores a goal in the same lap = "Frost Crown" worth 15 points
- Dropped ball on the course = nearest opponent claims possession
- Games are four 10-minute periods; unlimited rolling substitutions at the hub

**Key Skills Required:** Cross-country skiing endurance and technique, lacrosse passing and shooting accuracy, gymnastics bar and trampoline execution under cold-weather fatigue

FCP rewards teams that manage stamina across a grueling ski loop while executing precision throwing, clean gymnastics skills, and clinical lacrosse finishing — the Frost Crown is the sport's holy grail, demanding total mastery of all three disciplines in a single breathtaking lap.

**REEF COMBAT DECATHLON (RCD)**

RCD combines Triathlon, Judo, and Disc Golf.

**Setup:** A coastal course beginning with a 400m open-water swim, transitioning to a 2km beach/trail run, and ending at a 9-hole disc golf course laid out across sand dunes and rocky terrain. At three "Clash Circles" — padded mats embedded along the run route — judo bouts occur. Teams of 4 players each.

**Rules:**
- All players start simultaneously with the open-water swim; disc golf discs are worn in waterproof hip pouches
- **Triathlon element:** Players swim 400m, then run 2km to the disc golf course. Fastest team to place all 4 players at the disc golf starting hole earns a "Tide Advantage" — one free disc re-throw per player for the round
- **Judo element:** At each Clash Circle along the run, the first two opposing players to arrive simultaneously must stop for a 30-second judo bout (standard throws and takedowns, judged by a referee). A clean ippon throw = 5 points and the winner continues running immediately; the loser waits 20 seconds before resuming. A waza-ari (partial throw) = 2 points; both continue after 10 seconds
- **Disc Golf element:** All 9 holes are played under standard disc golf rules — fewest combined team throws wins the golf phase = 8 points. Each hole-in-one = 3 bonus points. A throw executed while still sprinting (no stopping) = "Reef Toss," worth double if it lands within 2 meters of the basket
- A player who wins both Clash Circles they encounter AND records a hole-in-one = "Reef Master," worth 10 bonus points
- Games are 2 full course circuits; cumulative score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Open-water swimming endurance, trail running speed under fatigue, judo explosive throwing technique, disc golf accuracy across varied terrain

RCD rewards athletes who can sustain cardiovascular output across swim and run phases, seize Clash Circle moments without losing race position, and maintain disc throwing precision while oxygen-depleted — with every Reef Toss and Reef Master moment capable of completely reshaping the leaderboard.

**IRONMESH HANDBALL RALLY (IHR)**

IHR combines Tennis, Handball, and Competitive Climbing.

**Setup:** A rectangular indoor court (30m x 15m) with a 6-foot net across the center. On each back wall, a climbing mesh panel (10m wide x 8m tall) with color-coded holds replaces the standard wall. A foam handball is used. Teams of 3 players each.

**Rules:**
- Players hit the ball over the net using closed-fist handball strikes (no rackets, no open palm) — the ball may bounce once on the floor before being returned (tennis bounce rule)
- Standard rally scoring: winning a rally = 1 point; games to 15, best of 5 games
- **Climbing element:** A player may retreat to their back wall and climb the mesh panel. Reaching a RED hold (top third) within one continuous climb earns a "Height Token." Falling off = no token, 5-second recovery pause
- Height Tokens are spent to activate a "Power Strike" — a handball smash that, if it wins the rally, scores 3 points instead of 1
- A player who climbs to a RED hold AND immediately descends to spike the ball within 8 seconds = "Mesh Slam," worth 4 points if it wins the rally
- Opponents may attempt to win the rally while a player is climbing — the climbing player's teammates must cover 2v3
- Touching the net = point conceded; climbing during your own service = illegal
- A between-the-legs handball strike that wins a rally = "Tunnel Shot," worth 2 points

**Key Skills Required:** Tennis-style court positioning and rally consistency, handball striking power and accuracy, rock climbing grip strength and speed under match pressure

IHR rewards teams that gamble on climbing bonuses at the right moment — sacrificing a 3v3 advantage for a single high-value strike — while maintaining relentless handball precision to punish opponents who climb at the wrong time.

**CANYON DRIFT PENTATHLON (CDP)**

CDP combines Whitewater Kayaking, Wrestling, and Shot Put.

**Setup:** A 600m river canyon course with Class III rapids, 8 slalom gates, and three "Plateau Zones" — wide calm pools where flat rocky platforms (10m x 8m) jut from the riverbank. Each Plateau Zone has a shot put circle (2.5m diameter) and a padded wrestling mat. A weighted foam "canyon ball" (5kg) sits at each platform. Teams of 4 players each in single kayaks.

**Rules:**
- Players paddle the full canyon course navigating all 8 slalom gates — each missed gate = 5-second time penalty added to their run
- **Wrestling element:** At each Plateau Zone, the first opposing players to arrive simultaneously must beach their kayaks and engage in a 45-second freestyle wrestling bout on the mat. A takedown = 2 points; a pin (3-second hold) = 5 points and the loser waits 25 seconds before re-launching. If only one team arrives, they earn 2 free points
- **Shot Put element:** The wrestling winner earns one throw of the canyon ball from the shot put circle toward a floating target ring (15m downstream). Bullseye = 5 points; outer ring = 2 points; a throw released while mid-air off a platform edge = "Canyon Heave," doubling the value
- **Kayaking element:** Team with the lowest combined course time across all 4 members earns 6 bonus points per round
- A player who wins a wrestling pin AND lands a bullseye throw AND posts the fastest individual run = "Canyon Legend," worth 15 points
- Games are 3 full course runs; highest cumulative score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Whitewater kayaking precision and gate navigation, wrestling explosive takedown technique and mat control, shot put power and accuracy while fatigued mid-course

CDP rewards athletes who can sustain technical paddling under pressure, dominate opponents in sudden physical confrontations, and deliver explosive throwing accuracy — every Plateau Zone is a self-contained battle that can erase a comfortable paddling lead in seconds.

**SKYFORGE RELAY COMBAT (SRC)**

SRC combines Gymnastics, Rugby Sevens, and Competitive Archery.

**Setup:** A rectangular grass field (80m x 50m) with a gymnastics tumbling runway (25m long, spring floor surface) running down each sideline. Two archery target walls (120cm bullseye rings, sensor-equipped) stand at each end zone, 20m behind the try line. Teams of 6 players each.

**Rules:**
- Players advance a foam rugby ball by running and passing backward only (rugby rules); forward passes = turnover
- **Scoring:**
  - Grounding the ball over the opponent's try line = 3 points
  - Firing a foam-tipped arrow (bows stored at each end zone) into the target wall bullseye = 4 points; outer ring = 2 points
  - A try scored by a player who completed a tumbling pass (at least one aerial skill — cartwheel, round-off, or flip) down the sideline runway before receiving the scoring pass = "Forge Try" worth 6 points

- **Archery element:** Only the player who last touched the ball before a turnover or score may attempt an arrow shot. They have 10 seconds to reach the bow station and fire. Defenders may rush to physically block the shot lane (no contact with the archer allowed)

- **Gymnastics element:** Any player may enter the tumbling runway at any time, but while on it they cannot carry the ball. Completing a recognized skill sequence (minimum 2 connected elements) earns a "Spring Token" — redeemable to nullify one opponent tackle attempt in the next 60 seconds

- A player who tumbles, receives the ball, scores a try, AND the team immediately follows with a bullseye arrow in the same possession = **"Skyforge Surge"** worth 15 points

- Standard tackles (two-hand tag below the shoulders) force an immediate pass; three consecutive clean tags on the same ball carrier = turnover

- Games are two 15-minute halves; highest score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Rugby sevens speed, evasion, and passing vision; gymnastics aerial body control and tumbling precision; archery accuracy under time pressure and defensive interference

SRC rewards teams that chain gymnastics athleticism into scoring sequences while managing the risk-reward of committing a player to the archery station — every Skyforge Surge is a full-team masterpiece that can overturn a match in a single breathtaking possession.

**IRON TIDE KABADDI LUGE (ITKL)**

ITKL combines Kabaddi, Skeleton/Luge, and Water Polo.

**Setup:** A refrigerated 400m downhill ice track curves into an indoor aquatic arena at its base. The pool (25m x 15m) sits at the bottom of the track. A raised "Raid Platform" (12m x 8m, padded) spans the pool's center at waterline height, accessible from both pool and track. Two water polo goals (3m x 0.9m) float at opposite pool ends. Teams of 6 players each — 3 "Sliders" and 3 "Pool Raiders."

**Rules:**
- **Skeleton element:** Sliders ride skeleton sleds down the ice track headfirst. Each clean run (no wall contact) = 2 points. The fastest slider each round earns a "Speed Token" for their team
- **Kabaddi element:** One Pool Raider per team crosses the Raid Platform into opponent territory, chanting "kabaddi kabaddi" continuously, attempting to tag defenders and return safely. A successful raid = 1 point per tag + 1 bonus for safe return. Stopped by two defenders holding them = eliminated for 60 seconds
- **Water Polo element:** Non-raiding Pool Raiders swim and advance a water polo ball, shooting at the floating goal = 3 points. Defenders may block shots and intercept passes
- Speed Tokens are spent to grant the Raider a 10-second immunity from being held
- A Slider who finishes their run AND immediately dives into the pool to intercept a water polo pass = "Tide Entry," earning 3 bonus points if successful
- A Raider who tags 3+ defenders AND their team scores a water polo goal in the same possession = "Iron Surge," worth 8 points
- Roles swap (Sliders ↔ Pool Raiders) every 10 minutes
- Games are four 8-minute quarters; highest score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Skeleton speed and aerodynamic ice navigation, kabaddi breath control and evasive agility, water polo swimming endurance and shooting accuracy

ITKL rewards teams that synchronize blazing track runs with tactical raiding and clinical water polo — every Speed Token changes the kabaddi calculus, and every Iron Surge can erase a four-possession deficit in a single chaotic, glorious sequence.

**PINNACLE CROSS DUEL (PCD)**

PCD combines Bobsled, Javelin Throw, and Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu.

**Setup:** A 1,200m outdoor bobsled track with three "Pinnacle Stations" — wide flat platforms carved into the hillside at the track's curves. Each station has a padded grappling mat (8m x 8m) and a javelin launch zone with a floating target ring (1m diameter) tethered 25m downhill. Teams of 4 players each riding two-person bobs.

**Rules:**
- **Bobsled element:** Teams race two-person bobs down the full track in relay — each clean run (no wall scrape) = 3 points. The team posting the fastest combined run time each round earns a "Pinnacle Token"
- **Javelin element:** At each Pinnacle Station, one player exits the bob, retrieves a foam-tipped javelin, and hurls it at the floating target ring below. Bullseye = 5 points; outer ring = 2 points; a throw released while leaping off the platform edge = "Cliff Hurl," doubling the value if it scores
- **BJJ element:** If opposing players arrive at the same Pinnacle Station within 10 seconds of each other, they must engage in a 45-second BJJ grappling bout on the mat before throwing. A submission = 6 points and the winner throws first; a positional advantage (mount/back control at time) = 3 points. The loser must wait 20 seconds before throwing
- A Pinnacle Token is spent to allow a second javelin attempt at any station that round
- A player who wins a BJJ submission AND lands a bullseye throw AND their bob posts the fastest run = **"Pinnacle Legend"** worth 18 points
- Games are 3 full track circuits; highest cumulative score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Bobsled driving precision and aerodynamic technique, javelin throwing power and accuracy mid-course, BJJ ground control and submission hunting under cardiovascular fatigue

PCD rewards teams that balance blistering track speed with composed grappling and explosive throwing accuracy — every Pinnacle Station is a sudden gladiatorial interruption that can shatter a comfortable lead, and a single Pinnacle Legend performance can decide the entire match.

**MONSOON VAULT SUMO TENNIS (MVST)**

MVST combines Trampolining, Sumo Wrestling, and Badminton.

**Setup:** A circular court (25m diameter) with a springy rubberized floor surface. A badminton-height net (5ft) bisects the court. On each side, two embedded circular trampolines (3m diameter) are sunk flush into the floor near the back boundary. A central "Clash Ring" (4m diameter, raised 1ft with padded edges) sits directly on the net line, straddling both sides. Teams of 3 players each.

**Rules:**
- Players hit a lightweight foam shuttlecock over the net using open-palm strikes only (no rackets) — one bounce on the floor allowed before return (modified badminton)
- **Trampolining element:** A player who launches from a trampoline before striking the shuttlecock earns a "Sky Hit." A Sky Hit that wins the rally = 2 points instead of 1. A Sky Hit with a recognized aerial skill (tuck, pike, twist) = "Signature Sky Hit" worth 3 points, judged by a sideline referee
- **Sumo element:** Any player may step into the Clash Ring and issue a challenge. The nearest opponent must accept within 6 seconds or concede 2 points. Standard sumo rules — push opponent out of the ring or force any body part other than feet to touch the ring floor = win. Winner earns a "Dominance Token"; loser sits out 30 seconds
- Dominance Tokens are spent to make the next rally's winning point worth double
- Scoring: rally point system, games to 15, best of 5 games per match
- A player who launches from a trampoline, performs a full backflip, wins the rally, AND their teammate wins a simultaneous Clash Ring bout = "Monsoon Moment" worth 10 points
- Stepping off the court boundary while in possession = shuttle conceded
- A behind-the-back palm strike from a trampoline = "Blind Vault," worth 4 points if it wins the rally

**Key Skills Required:** Trampolining aerial awareness and trick execution, sumo leverage and explosive pushing technique, badminton-style hand-eye coordination and court coverage

MVST rewards teams that time their trampoline launches to create high-value Sky Hits while strategically deploying Clash Ring challenges to earn Dominance Tokens — every Monsoon Moment is a full-team spectacle that fuses gravity-defying acrobatics with raw physical dominance and razor-sharp racket sport instincts.

**TUNDRA SIEGE PENTATHLON (TSP)**

TSP combines Ice Climbing, Biathlon, and Water Polo.

**Setup:** A frozen outdoor complex featuring a 30m artificial ice climbing wall at each end, a 300m cross-country ski loop with two rifle stations, and a central heated pool (25m x 12m) with two floating water polo goals. Teams of 5 players each — 2 "Climbers," 2 "Skiers," and 1 "Pool Anchor" per possession, with roles rotating every 6 minutes.

**Rules:**
- **Ice Climbing element:** Climbers race up their team's ice wall using picks and crampons. The first to summit and ring a bell earns a "Summit Token." Falling = restart from the base. Both climbers may race simultaneously
- **Biathlon element:** Skiers loop the 300m course continuously. At each rifle station, they stop and throw a foam ball at 5 metal targets (biathlon-style). Each hit = 1 point; a miss = 20-second penalty loop. A clean sweep of all 5 targets = 4 bonus points
- **Water Polo element:** Pool Anchors swim and advance a water polo ball, shooting at floating goals = 3 points. Defenders block and intercept; no physical contact above the waterline
- Summit Tokens are spent to grant the Pool Anchor a 15-second "free zone" — all defenders must retreat 4m
- A Skier who completes a clean sweep AND their Pool Anchor scores in the same 60-second window = "Tundra Strike" worth 8 points
- A Climber who summits, leaps from the wall into the pool, and immediately intercepts a pass = "Ice Dive," earning 4 bonus points if successful
- Games are four 8-minute quarters; highest score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Ice climbing grip strength and vertical speed, biathlon skiing endurance and precision target throwing, water polo swimming stamina and shooting accuracy

TSP rewards teams that coordinate three simultaneous threat vectors — summit pressure, rifle station dominance, and pool control — with every Tundra Strike and Ice Dive capable of swinging momentum in a single electrifying sequence.

**IRONWAVE KITE COMBAT (IKC)**

IKC combines Kiteboarding/Kitesurfing, Fencing, and Volleyball.

**Setup:** A coastal lagoon (200m x 100m) with a central floating platform "Arena" (20m x 12m, non-slip surface, anchored at mid-water). A volleyball net (7ft high) bisects the platform. Two sensor-equipped fencing strips (8m long) run parallel to the net on each side of the platform. Buoy gates mark a kiteboarding course around the lagoon perimeter. Teams of 4 players each — 2 "Kite Riders" and 2 "Platform Players" per team, roles rotating every 8 minutes.

**Rules:**
- **Kiteboarding element:** Kite Riders harness-launch across the lagoon, navigating 6 buoy gates per circuit. Each clean circuit (no missed gates) = 2 points. The team completing the most circuits in a period earns a "Wind Token." A Kite Rider who lands a jump trick (grab, rotation) judged by a referee = "Air Bonus" worth 1 extra point
- **Volleyball element:** Platform Players volley a foam ball over the net — standard rally rules (3 touches max). Winning a rally = 1 point. A spike from a jump = 2 points. A Kite Rider may beach their board on the platform and sub in for one rally before returning to water
- **Fencing element:** Once per possession, a Platform Player may step onto their fencing strip and challenge an opponent. 30-second épée bout (sensor-equipped, padded tips). First to 3 touches wins = 3 points; a touch scored while lunging backward = "Retreat Riposte" worth 5 points. Loser sits out 40 seconds
- Wind Tokens are spent to allow a Kite Rider to crash-land directly onto the platform and spike the volleyball uncontested — "Kite Spike" worth 4 points
- A team that wins a fencing bout, wins the simultaneous volleyball rally, AND has a Kite Rider complete a clean circuit in the same 60-second window = **"Ironwave Surge"** worth 12 points
- Games are four 8-minute quarters; highest score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Kiteboarding wave-reading, harness control, and aerial trick execution; volleyball court awareness and spiking timing; fencing blade precision and composure after high-intensity water riding

IKC rewards teams that coordinate wind-powered speed with platform combat and net skill — every Wind Token creates a spectacular Kite Spike opportunity, every fencing duel reshapes possession, and a single Ironwave Surge can overturn a match in one breathtaking multi-discipline sequence.

**OBSIDIAN TRACK WRESTLING GOLF (OTWG)**

OTWG combines Hammer Throw (Track & Field), Greco-Roman Wrestling, and Golf.

**Setup:** An 18-station outdoor course (2km total) across varied terrain — fairways, rough, elevation changes. Each station has a circular throwing cage (hammer throw), a padded wrestling circle (8m diameter), and a sunken target hole with a flagstick. Teams of 4 players each.

**Rules:**
- Players walk/jog the course carrying a modified hammer (padded, 3kg) and a single flat-faced club (like a mallet)
- **Golf element:** At each station, players strike a foam ball toward the target hole using the mallet. Fewest combined team strokes across all 18 stations = 6 bonus points. A hole-in-one = 3 bonus points
- **Hammer Throw element:** Before putting, each player must enter the throwing cage and hurl their hammer toward a distance marker. Reaching the 30m marker = "Power Clear" — the player may remove one opponent's ball from the hole and reset it 5m back. Reaching 40m = "Obsidian Throw," granting a free stroke mulligan
- **Wrestling element:** If two opposing players arrive at a station within 15 seconds of each other, they must enter the wrestling circle for a 40-second Greco-Roman bout before throwing or putting. A clean suplex or throw = 4 points; controlling the bout at time = 2 points. The winner chooses throwing order that station
- A player who wins a wrestling bout, hits the 40m marker, AND holes out in one stroke = **"Obsidian Crown"** worth 15 points
- Games are 18 stations; cumulative score wins

**Key Skills Required:** Golf precision and course management, hammer throw rotational power and distance, Greco-Roman wrestling upper-body control and explosive throwing technique

OTWG rewards athletes who combine the patience of a golfer, the explosive power of a field thrower, and the physical dominance of a wrestler — every station is a three-discipline gauntlet where a single Obsidian Crown can shatter any opponent's lead.

**VORTEX POLO SPRINT (VPS)**

VPS combines Canoe Slalom, Lacrosse, and Gymnastics Floor Exercise.

**Setup:** An indoor arena with a 150m artificial whitewater channel snaking through the center, 10 hanging slalom gates overhead.
